title: blueprint_portal.txt
text:
﻿
general info & description
This document describes the blueprint for the secure publication of web-based applications of Customer Company 40 over the Internet.
The architecture is based on a Reverse Proxy and Web Application Firewall (WAF) concept with TLS termination at the perimeter level. The objective is to protect internal application servers from direct Internet exposure while ensuring secure and controlled communication with external users.
The Web Publishing architecture is used for the following applications:
• Customer portal (portal.kf40.ch)
• Partner access
• Internal web services with external interface
Central principles:
• No direct publication of internal servers
• TLS termination exclusively on the WAF
• Backend servers are located in a separate server VLAN
• HTTPS semantics must be preserved for the backend
• Standardized templates for all portal applications
Best practices:
• Use of dedicated WAF templates per application
• Documented header rewrite rules
• No use of generic “Default-Strict” profiles for productive portals
• Validation against this blueprint after every security patch
MUST BE OBSERVED:
All changes to WAF profiles, TLS policies, or Reverse Proxy rules must be validated against this document.
design
The architecture consists of three logical zones:
Internet
→ Perimeter Zone (WAF / Reverse Proxy)
→ Internal Application Zone
Components
1. Web Application Firewall (WAF)
o TLS termination
o Inspection policy
o Header rewrite
o Load balancing
2. Reverse Proxy Layer
o Forwarding to application servers
o Host header handling
o Session persistence
3. Backend Application Server
o Expects HTTPS semantics
o No direct Internet access
o Internal authentication
TLS Offloading
TLS is terminated on the WAF.
Communication between the WAF and backend is performed over HTTP by default.
In order for the backend to correctly interpret the original HTTPS request, the following headers must be set:
• X-Forwarded-Proto: https
• X-Forwarded-Host
These headers must not be removed or overwritten by inspection or security profiles.
WAF Template
For portal applications, the template:
Portal-App-Template
must be used.
This template contains:
• Header rewrite rules
• HTTPS semantics preservation
• HTTP/2 compatibility configuration
• Session handling
• Adjustments for authentication flows
The use of other templates (e.g., Default-Strict) is not permitted for productive portal services.
After Security Patches
After firmware or security updates, it is mandatory to verify:
• Whether the correct template is still active
• Whether custom rewrite rules remain in place
• Whether inspection policies have not been reset
• Whether TLS profiles remain unchanged
decision tree & use cases
1. Question: Is the application reachable internally but not externally?
Yes → Proceed to question 2.
No → The issue is likely not within the Web Publishing layer.
2. Question: Is a 502 or 503 error displayed during external access?
Yes → Check WAF template and header rewrite rules.
No → Proceed to question 3.
3. Question: Is there a redirect loop between HTTP and HTTPS?
Yes → Check X-Forwarded-Proto header.
No → Proceed to question 4.
4. Question: Was a certificate recently renewed or a security patch applied?
Yes → Validate configuration according to section “After Security Patches”.
No → Proceed to question 5.
5. Question: Is TLS inspection enabled?
Yes → Verify that the Portal-App-Template is used.
No → Check backend health status.

Subject: Urgent: Emails remain in Outbox – KF22
From: Contact Person 08
To: HELPDESK, Consulting Sample Company
Hello everyone
Since this morning, emails are stuck in the Outbox for several users. In some cases, we receive the following error message:
0x800CCC0E – Connection to the server failed
SMTP server not responding
Approximately 12 employees from the Sales team are affected.
Internet connectivity is generally working.
Could you please check this urgently?
Kind regards
Contact Person 08
14.01.25 08:21
Ticket creation: TI-1548823 | KF22: Exchange Online – Emails remain in Outbox
Ticket notification sent to Contact Person 08
Priority: High
14.01.25 08:29
Ticket assigned: Employee 34
Status changes
14.01.25 08:35
Status: In Progress
Notes:
14.01.25 08:42 – Employee 34
Contact established with Contact Person 08.
Additional information:
• Issue affects only users with locally installed Outlook (Windows 11)
• Webmail (OWA) works
• Mobile devices (iOS) work
• Error occurs only when sending
• Receiving works
Initial assessment:
→ Likely SMTP authentication or Hybrid Connector issue
14.01.25 09:15 – Client-side troubleshooting
Actions performed:
• Recreated Outlook profile
• Removed and re-added account
• Checked SMTP port (587 with STARTTLS)
• Disabled antivirus SMTP scanning
• Checked client firewall
Result: No improvement
14.01.25 10:30 – Exchange Admin Center analysis
• Checked Mailflow → Queue Monitoring
• No global Microsoft 365 outage reported
• Performed Message Trace → no blocking visible
• SMTP AUTH globally disabled? → No
Suspicion: Hybrid environment with local Exchange relay
14.01.25 11:12 – Escalation
Ticket forwarded to System Engineer (Exchange Specialist).
Reason:
Possible misconfiguration in Hybrid Connector or SMTP Auth policy
14.01.25 11:15
Ticket no longer in your responsibility | TI-1548823
Forwarded to: BU Infrastructure Engineering
14.01.25 11:16
Ticket assigned: System Engineer 07
Further analysis (System Engineer)
15.01.25 08:55 – System Engineer 07
Hybrid setup analysis:
• Environment: Exchange Hybrid (OnPrem + M365)
• Send Connector checked
• TLS certificate checked
• SPF / DKIM unchanged
Log analysis on Exchange Server:
Event Log shows:
535 5.7.3 Authentication unsuccessful
SMTP AUTH was disabled for the affected users.
15.01.25 10:20 – Root cause identified
Tenant security policy was adjusted last week:
Security Default → enabled
Result: SMTP AUTH disabled for legacy clients
Sales team still uses older Outlook version (Build 2019 MSI).
15.01.25 11:05 – Actions
• SMTP AUTH selectively enabled for affected users
• Conditional Access Policy adjusted
• Test sending successful
16.01.25 09:00 – Monitoring
Mailflow monitored for 24 hours
No further errors in logs
21.01.25 14:12 – Customer feedback
All users can send normally again.
No further issues observed.
Ticket closure
22.01.25 16:42 – HELPDESK
Dear Contact Person 08
Your ticket dated 14.01.25 has been successfully closed.
Root cause:
SMTP AUTH was disabled due to new Security Default Policy.
Action taken:
Selective activation and adjustment of the Conditional Access Policy.
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to contact us.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company – Helpdesk

title: Blueprint NDES_Intune.txt
text:
﻿General Information and Description
Certificate Services & Device Authentication
Microsoft NDES / Certificates / Intune / AD CS
Version / History
Version | Date | Author | Notes
0.1 | 11.02.2026 | PS | Creation (Initial)
0.2 | – | – | –
Microsoft Network Device Enrollment Service (NDES) is a role within Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS) and is used for the automated issuance of certificates for devices, services, and applications.
NDES is frequently used in combination with Microsoft Intune, network devices, or service-based authentication mechanisms.
Certificates are used to:
• Uniquely identify devices
• Enable secure communication
• Implement authentication processes without user interaction
In complex environments, issues can arise when:
• Certificates are expired or invalid
• Templates are misconfigured
• Communication paths between NDES, the CA, and target systems are disrupted
Since certificates are often used in the background, errors frequently appear indirectly, for example in the form of connection drops, authentication failures, or non-transparent service outages.
This blueprint describes the fundamental functionality of NDES-based certificate services and their integration into hybrid IT architectures.
Licensing
The following fundamentals apply for the use of this architecture:
• Windows Server license for AD CS and NDES
• Microsoft Intune license (optional, for device integration)
• No separate license required for AD CS or NDES
Licensing depends on whether certificates are issued for users, devices, or services, and whether cloud-based management is used.
Best Practices
Our best practices are based on official Microsoft recommendations:
• Clear separation of Root CA and Issuing CA
• Regular review of certificate validity periods
• Documentation of all certificate templates
• Restriction of permissions on NDES
• Monitoring of certificate requests and errors







Design





Decision Tree & Use Cases
Network Device Enrollment Service (NDES)
Decision Tree – NDES Installation & Operation
Question 1
Is the Network Device Enrollment Service (NDES) generally installed and reachable?
• Yes: Proceed to Question 2.
• No: Install NDES and restart the decision process.
Question 2
Does the issue already occur when starting the NDES service?
• Yes: Proceed to Question 3.
• No: Proceed to Question 4.
Question 3
Are all IIS components required for NDES installed
(ASP.NET 3.5, ASP.NET 4.7.2, Request Filtering, IIS 6 Management Compatibility)?
• Yes: Proceed to Question 5.
• No:
→ See screenshot “Install the NDES service”
Install the missing components and test again.
Question 4
Has the AD CS post-configuration for NDES been completed in full?
• Yes: Proceed to Question 5.
• No:
→ See screenshot “Configure the NDES service”
Complete the post-configuration and test again.
Question 5
Is the NDES service account configured correctly
(e.g., service account assigned, member of IIS_IUSRS)?
• Yes: Proceed to Question 6.
• No:
→ See screenshot “Install the NDES service”
Adjust the service account and test again.
Question 6
Is the correct Issuing Certification Authority and the appropriate certificate template configured?
• Yes: Proceed to Question 7.
• No:
→ See screenshot “Configure the NDES service”
Select the correct CA and verify the certificate template.
Question 7
Can certificates be successfully issued via NDES?
• Yes:
→ Document the configuration and close the ticket.
• No:
→ Escalate the case to AD CS or infrastructure specialists.

text:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF11: Intune NDES Connector lost communication
Ticket number: TI-722423
Company: Customer Company 11
Contact person: Contact Person 8
Owner: Employee 11
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 27.03.24 12:04
Closure
Closure date: 14.06.24 16:10
Closure text: Incident successfully resolved
Activities
Emails:
27.03.24 13:10 | TI-722423 | KF11: Intune NDES Connector lost communication | Employee 11 | Contact Person 8
Hello Contact Person 8, hello Contact Person 2,
Together with the network team, I adjusted the firewall rules and then reinstalled the connector – this time without proxy configuration. After the reinstallation, the connector was displayed as “green” again in Azure (this step is required so that it can properly communicate with the Intune service).
Since we had identified login issues, we temporarily excluded the service account from the MFA requirement.
After completing the installation, I removed the “Intune Admin” role from the service account again. If we need to reinstall the connector, this role must be temporarily reassigned.
During the installation, I followed the Microsoft guide
“Configure infrastructure to support SCEP certificate profiles with Microsoft Intune.”
Attached you will find logs to quickly verify whether the connector is operating correctly. See blueprint_1.
Please do not make any changes to the settings without prior coordination.
If issues occur during the Intune deployment, you may contact the Helpdesk or the WPS team at any time.

title: Ticketzusammenfassung_teams1.txt
text:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls
Ticket number: TI-1602241
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 14
Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 08.07.25 08:21
Closure
Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05
Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again.
Activities
08.07.25 08:10
Call from Mr. Contact Person 14.
He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”.
Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected.
08.07.25 08:21
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33.
08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14
Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14
Thank you for your report.
Could you please briefly confirm the following points:
– Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)?
– Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected?
– Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones?
– Since when has the issue existed?
Thank you for your feedback.
Kind regards
08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14
Good morning
Yes, it affects only external calls.
Both incoming and outgoing.
At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location.
Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday.
Kind regards
08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note
Test call performed with user S. Meier.
Internal Teams call works.
External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing.
Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message.
08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer
We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test.
Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical?
08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response
We tested with three additional persons.
Same behavior everywhere.
Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly).
LAN connection.
Home office users do not seem to be affected.
09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again
Good morning
We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend.
Could this have an impact?
Kind regards
09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note
Firewall change timing plausible.
Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team.
09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18
09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration.
Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established.
We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream).
We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings.
Kind regards
10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis
SBC shows successful SIP connections.
No call drops.
Media negotiation (SDP) is performed.
Notable:
RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction.
10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison
New rule implemented at Zurich location.
Outbound traffic configured more restrictively.
Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges.
10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase
Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports.
Test call performed.
Audio immediately available in both directions.
Firewall reverted to restrictive rule.
Issue occurs again.
11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer
Good day
We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location.
Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked.
We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification.
We will provide an update shortly.
Kind regards
14.07.25 – Internal documentation review
Comparison with internal document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1”
Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled.
Finding:
Zurich location configured with Media Bypass.
Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range.
16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment
Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
17.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We tested several external calls.
Audio works normally again.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you for your support.
Kind regards
18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 14
The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment.
The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification.
Voice transmission is now stable again.
Kind regards

Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External users permanently displayed as “Offline”
Ticket number: TI-1607314

Closure text: Analysis completed. Configuration adjusted according to Teams Federation Blueprint. Presence display correct again.
Activities
12.08.25 08:32 – Email from Contact Person 18
Good morning
Since last week, our external project partners in Microsoft Teams are permanently displayed as “Offline”, although they state that they are online.
Chats are partially delayed, calls are sometimes not signaled.
Internal users see each other correctly.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
12.08.25 08:47
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 37.
12.08.25 09:20 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Does this affect all external organizations or only specific partners?
Were any recent changes made to Teams or security policies?
Thank you.
12.08.25 10:05 – Customer response
It mainly affects partner company “TechBuild AG”.
With other companies, it appears to work.
Two weeks ago, we revised our Teams security policies (made external communication more restrictive).
12.08.25 11:40 – Internal analysis
Test with TechBuild AG:
• Presence displayed as “Offline”
• Chat delivered
• Status does not change
• External calls sometimes do not ring
Test with another partner company → works normally.
13.08.25 08:55 – Email to customer
Good morning
We are currently reviewing the Federation and External Access configuration.
Can you confirm whether the SIP domain of TechBuild AG has recently changed?
Kind regards
13.08.25 10:22 – Customer response
No, the domain remains unchanged.
However, we noticed that TechBuild recently migrated to Direct Routing.
13.08.25 14:10 – Teams Admin Center analysis
External Access Policy reviewed.
Domain techbuild.ch is still allowed.
However, in the new policy, “Presence sharing” was set to “Limited”.
Correlation unclear.
14.08.25 09:30 – Internal log analysis
Teams PowerShell checked:
Get-CsTenantFederationConfiguration
Observation:
AllowPublicUsers = False
AllowFederatedUsers = True
However, “EnablePresencePrivacyMode” is active.
14.08.25 11:45 – Comparison with internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Federation & External Access – Version 1.4”
Section 4 describes:
• Difference between Federation and Presence Sharing
• Impact of Privacy Mode
• Behavior with restrictive policies
• Required settings for partners using Direct Routing
15.08.25 – Internal test
Temporary adjustment of presence settings in test tenant.
Test with external domain.
Presence displayed correctly.
Reverted to production configuration →
External presence again permanently “Offline”.
18.08.25 – Coordination with customer
New security policy introduced to restrict external presence information.
This policy conflicts with Federation standard.
19.08.25 – Adjustment
Configuration adjusted according to Federation Blueprint.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
21.08.25 – Customer feedback
External partners are again correctly displayed as “Available” or “Busy”.
Chats arrive without delay.
Calls are signaled again.
22.08.25 16:12 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 18
The root cause was a configuration deviation in the area of Federation and Presence Sharing following a policy adjustment.
The configuration was corrected according to the internal Teams Federation architecture.
Presence display is now functioning correctly again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Internal Teams calls function normally.
External calls are terminated immediately; in some cases the message “Call failed” appears.

It affects about 6 users, all from the Sales team.

It affects all external numbers.
We are not aware of any license changes.
03.09.25 11:15 – Internal note

Internal call → works.
External call → immediate termination after dialing.
Teams briefly shows “Connecting”, then “Call ended”.
04.09.25 08:50 – Email to customer
Good morning
We are currently reviewing the voice routing and license configuration.
Can you confirm whether these users were recently moved to another team or security group?
Kind regards
04.09.25 09:30 – Customer response
Yes, the affected users were organizationally reassigned to the new department “Enterprise Sales” last week.
New Azure AD groups were created for this purpose.
04.09.25 11:40 – Teams Admin Center analysis
Users have:
• Teams license
• Calling Plan not active
• Direct Routing configured
Observation:
Voice Routing Policy = “Global”

05.09.25 09:05 – Internal review

Policy “DR-Standard” includes PSTN Usage “External-Route”.
05.09.25 11:20 – Email to customer
Good day

08.09.25 – Internal analysis
New Azure AD group “Enterprise-Sales” was created.
Automated provisioning script assigns policy “Global” by default when users become members of this group.
The internal document
“Blueprint – Teams Voice Routing & Policy Assignment – Version 1.3”
states that users with Direct Routing must be assigned a dedicated routing policy.
09.09.25 – Test phase
Temporary assignment of policy “DR-Standard” to affected user.
Test call to mobile phone performed.
Call successful.
Reverted to “Global” → issue reproducible.
10.09.25 – Coordination with Identity team
Provisioning script was modified two weeks ago.
New group structure was not reflected in the routing blueprint.
11.09.25 – Adjustment
Policy assignment corrected according to Voice Routing architecture.
Provisioning logic adjusted.
12.09.25 – Customer feedback
Good day
All affected users can dial external numbers again.
Longer calls also function normally.
Thank you for your support.
12.09.25 15:40 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 21
The root cause was an incorrect Voice Routing Policy assignment following an organizational restructuring.
The routing configuration was adjusted according to the internal Teams Voice Architecture.
External calls are now possible again.

18.12.24 00:33 Ticket closed | TI-1385257
Dear Employee 14,
Your ticket from 26.11.24 10:07 has been closed.
Closure description:
Parameters adjusted on ADFS as in the TEST environment.

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

title: Blueprint_teams.txt
text:
﻿
general info & description
Microsoft Teams Federation & External Access – Customer Company 52
Version: 1.0
Date: 25.08.2025
Author: Unified Communications / M365 Engineering
Notes: Initial creation based on federation specifications and security policies KF52
This document describes the architectural and configuration specifications for Microsoft Teams Federation, External Access and Presence Sharing within Customer Company 52 AG.
The objective is secure, controlled and functional communication with external organizations via Microsoft Teams while considering security and data protection requirements.
The scope includes:
• Federation with external Microsoft 365 tenants
• Presence display of external users
• Chat and call signaling
• Policies for External Access
• Privacy and Presence settings
Core principles:
• Federation is domain-based
• Presence information is released in a controlled manner
• Security policies must not unintentionally restrict federation functionality
• Changes to External Access policies must be validated for architectural compliance
IMPORTANT:
Restrictions in the area of Presence Sharing or Privacy Mode may impact:
• Presence display
• Call signaling
• Delivery of chat messages
• Availability synchronization
without federation itself being disabled.
design
Architecture overview
Internal user (Tenant KF52)
→ Teams Cloud Service
→ Federation Layer
→ External tenant
Communication is based on:
• SIP federation
• Presence exchange
• Tenant policy evaluation
Federation configuration
The federation configuration is based on:
• AllowFederatedUsers
• AllowPublicUsers
• AllowedDomains (if restrictions are enabled)
If domain restrictions are enabled, partner domains must be explicitly allowed.
Presence Sharing
Presence information can be influenced by the following parameters:
• EnablePresencePrivacyMode
• PresenceSharingSetting
• External access policies at user level
Presence Sharing can operate in the following modes:
• Full Presence
• Limited Presence
• Disabled
In Limited or Privacy mode, external users may appear as “Offline” even though chat remains possible.
Policy dependencies
Changes to the following configurations may indirectly affect federation:
• Teams Messaging Policies
• External Access Policies
• Conditional Access Policies
• Privacy Mode settings
• Tenant federation configuration
Security adjustments must not be performed without alignment with this blueprint.
decision tree & use cases
1. Question: Does chat with external users generally work?
No → Check federation configuration.
Yes → Proceed to question 2.
2. Question: Are external users permanently displayed as “Offline”?
Yes → Check Presence Sharing settings.
No → Further analysis required.
3. Question: Was a security or External Access policy recently modified?
Yes → Perform validation against blueprint design.
No → Proceed to question 4.
4. Question: Is EnablePresencePrivacyMode enabled?
Yes → Review impact on external presence display.
No → Proceed to question 5.
5. Question: Is the affected partner domain explicitly allowed?
No → Check domain configuration.
Yes → Compare cross-tenant policies.

15.01.26 08:45 – Email from Contact Person 28
Good morning
We have noticed that larger scanned documents are not delivered via email.
Small documents (1–2 pages) work normally.
Larger PDFs (approx. 20–30 pages) are not delivered internally or externally.
No clear error message appears on the printer.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
15.01.26 08:57
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 34.
15.01.26 09:30 – Initial analysis
Test scan with one page → delivery successful.
Test scan with 25 pages → no delivery.
Printer displays “Send successful”, but no email in inbox.
15.01.26 10:20 – Mail log review
Internal mail server logs show no incoming message for large files.
SMTP relay log shows entry “Message size exceeds fixed limit”.
16.01.26 09:05 – Inquiry to customer
Were there any recent changes to the mail environment or security policies?
16.01.26 10:10 – Customer response
Two weeks ago, the maximum email size was reduced to limit spam.
We were not aware that this could impact the printers.
17.01.26 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail defines:
• Sending occurs via dedicated SMTP relay
• Maximum file size must be aligned with mail infrastructure
• Changes to mail server policies must be coordinated with scan systems
Finding:
New limit = 10 MB
Scanned PDFs exceed this limit at higher resolution.
20.01.26 – Further analysis
Scan settings on device checked.
Default resolution 600 dpi.
PDF compression disabled.
21.01.26 – Test phase
Temporary reduction of scan resolution to 300 dpi.
Test scan with 25 pages → successful.
Parallel coordination with mail team.
Maximum size moderately increased.
22.01.26 – Configuration adjustment
Scan profile adjusted according to blueprint:
• Default resolution reduced
• PDF compression enabled
• Mail limit adjusted
23.01.26 14:50 – Customer feedback
Several larger documents tested.
Internal and external delivery successful.
23.01.26 15:36 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 28
The root cause was a discrepancy between the scan configuration and the reduced maximum email size in the SMTP relay.
The settings were adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works reliably, including for larger documents.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

text:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds
Ticket number: TI-1604879
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 16
Owner: Employee 35
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 22.07.25 09:14
Closure
Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52
Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable.
Activities
22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk
Good morning
Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds.
The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time.
Internal Teams calls are not affected.
Could you please review this?
Kind regards
22.07.25 09:14
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35.
22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Thank you for your report.
Could you please confirm:
– Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls?
– Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration?
– Are all locations affected or only a specific one?
Thank you very much.
22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response
It only affects the Basel location.
Both incoming and outgoing calls.
The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier.
Home office users are not affected.
22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs.
Signaling is established correctly.
The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client.
Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location?
Kind regards
23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response
On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented.
A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created.
Could this be relevant?
24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis
SBC logs reviewed:
– SIP INVITE successful
– 200 OK correct
– Call established
– After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC
No obvious error code.
24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis
No blocking visible.
Ports open according to standard configuration.
24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis
Session-Expires header reviewed.
Session timer set to 30 seconds.
Comparison with other locations shows:
There, 180 seconds.
25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1”
Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant.
Note in the blueprint:
For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination.
25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests
Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC.
Test call performed.
Call remains stable for several minutes.
Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible.
28.07.25 – Coordination with network team
New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy.
UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.
Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds.
29.07.25 – Adjustment
NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
30.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We conducted several test calls.
No more disconnections observed.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you very much.
31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 16
The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Calls now remain stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

title: blueprint_print_scan.txt
text:
﻿general info & description
Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Customer Company
Version: 1.0
Date: 15.10.2025
Author: IT Infrastructure
Status: Approved
This document describes the standard for the print and scan infrastructure within the customer company.
The objective is a stable, centrally managed printing environment for all locations based on the following core principles:
• Central printer management via print servers
• Automatic printer assignment via Group Policy (GPO)
• Standardized driver versions
• Location-based printer concepts
• Uniform configuration for Scan-to-Mail
The print infrastructure is based on:
• Windows print servers
• Location-based printer queues
• User- or group-based assignment
• VLAN-separated printer networks
Manual installation of local printers is not intended.
design
Architecture overview
User device
→ Print server
→ Printer (in printer VLAN)
Printers are not directly accessible from the client but are addressed exclusively via the central print server.
Printer provisioning
Printers are:
• Created as a queue on the print server
• Configured with a standardized driver
• Assigned via GPO based on location or department
Default printers are set automatically.
Driver standard
Only approved and tested driver versions are used.
After Windows updates, it must be verified:
• Whether drivers remain compatible
• Whether Point-and-Print restrictions are active
• Whether printers are still mapped correctly
Scan-to-Mail
Scan-to-Mail is performed:
• Via a dedicated SMTP relay
• Using a configured service account
• Secured with TLS
Changes to mail servers or authentication mechanisms may affect scan functionality.
Location concept
Each location has:
• At least one default printer
• One multifunction printer with scan capability
• Assignment via AD groups
Printers are located in a separate printer VLAN.
 
decision tree & use cases
Decision Tree
Initial question:
Does the issue occur when printing or when scanning?
A) Printing affected
A1: Is the printer visible in Windows Device Manager or in “Printers & Scanners”?
• No →
Check whether the user is a member of the correct AD group.
If yes → Check whether the GPO has been applied.
If GPO not applied → Perform Group Policy update.
If GPO applied → Check reachability of the print server.
• Yes →
Proceed to A2.
A2: Is the print job displayed in the queue?
• No →
Issue is likely client-side (check driver or assignment).
• Yes →
Proceed to A3.
A3: Does the print job remain stuck in the queue?
• Yes →
Check whether the queue is paused.
If not → Check the Print Spooler service.
If Spooler is running → Check network connection to the printer (Ping/VLAN).
• No →
Proceed to A4.
A4: Does the issue affect only one location?
• Yes →
Check VLAN reachability.
Check DNS resolution of the printer.
Check location-based GPO assignment.
• No →
Check driver compatibility or print server service.
A5: Did the issue occur after a Windows update?
• Yes →
Check driver compatibility.
Check Point-and-Print policy.
Validate security policies.
• No →
Further analysis according to standard process.
B) Scanning affected
B1: Is printing still working?
• Yes →
Issue likely in Scan-to-Mail or destination path.
• No →
First analyze printing issue according to section A.
B2: Is an error message displayed during scanning?
• SMTP or connection error →
Check SMTP relay reachability.
Check service account authentication credentials.
Validate TLS configuration.
• No error message, but no delivery →
Check mail queue.
Check spam/filter rules.

title: blueprint_financialreport.txt
text:
﻿
general info & description
Management Reporting & Financial Reporting Infrastructure Standard
Version 1.0
Date: 12.04.2024
Status: Approved
This document describes the architecture, security configuration and operational standards for the Financial Reporting (Management Reporter / Financial Reports) infrastructure within the D365 Finance & Operations environment of Customer Company 11.
The objective is to ensure:
• Stable Report Designer startup
• Access to existing reports
• Consistent report metadata
• High-performance report generation
• TLS-compliant security configuration
The reporting infrastructure depends on:
• AOS servers
• Financial Reporting Data Mart
• IIS components
• TLS / Cipher Suite configuration
• Service accounts
• SQL backend
The system is configured consistently across environments (UAT, PROD).
design
Architecture overview
User (Browser / Report Designer)
→ AOS Server (Application Object Server)
→ Financial Reporting Services
→ Data Mart (SQL)
→ IIS / TLS Layer
Financial Reporting components
1. Financial Reporting Service
Hosts Report Designer and Report Rendering Engine
2. Data Mart
Provides aggregated financial data
3. AOS Server
Handles authentication and communication
4. IIS Layer
Responsible for TLS connections and Cipher Suites
Security concept
• TLS encryption according to company standard
• Only defined Cipher Suites allowed
• Best-practice configuration according to Microsoft recommendations
• Uniform configuration across all environments
Misconfigurations in TLS or Cipher Suites can result in:
• Report Designer starting extremely slowly
• Reports not loading
• Authors appearing as “unknown”
• Reports not being found
Operational principles
• Changes to Cipher Suites must be performed synchronously in all environments
• After adjustments, a restart of the AOS servers is required
• Data Mart reset is only relevant in case of data inconsistency
• Global issues usually indicate infrastructure or security layer problems
decision tree & use cases
Initial question
Does the issue occur only in one environment or globally (UAT + PROD)?
A) Issue only in one environment
1. Check Data Mart reset
2. Check SQL connection
3. Check AOS service
4. Check IIS configuration
B) Issue in multiple environments simultaneously
→ Infrastructure or security configuration likely
Further checks:
1. Were Cipher Suites changed?
2. Were TLS policies adjusted?
3. Was a Windows security update installed?
4. Were IIS settings modified?
C) Reports show “unknown” as author
1. Check service account connection
2. Check authentication handshake
3. Check TLS compatibility
D) Report Designer extremely slow
1. Test direct access via AOS Server
2. Check network latency
3. Check TLS/Cipher negotiation
E) Report not found
1. Check Data Mart consistency
2. Check report metadata
3. Check TLS communication between components

title: blueprint_Directory Domain Controller .txt
text:
﻿general info & description
Active Directory Domain Controller & Microsoft Defender for Identity Standard
Version 1.0
Date: 10.10.2024
Status: Approved
This document describes the architecture, security requirements and operational standards for Domain Controllers with installed Microsoft Defender for Identity Sensor within Customer Company 10.
The objective is:
• Stable monitoring of Active Directory activities
• Proper functionality of the Defender for Identity sensors
• Ensuring the necessary network and firewall permissions
• Standardized configuration across all environments (On-Prem and Azure DCs)
Microsoft Defender for Identity sensors run directly on the Domain Controllers and analyze:
• Authentication processes
• Kerberos and NTLM traffic
• LDAP communication
• Replication behavior
Misconfigurations typically result in:
• Sensor status “unhealthy”
• Connection errors to the Defender Cloud Service
• Missing security alerts
• Recurring health alerts



design
Architecture overview
Domain Controller (On-Prem / Azure)
→ Defender for Identity Sensor
→ Windows Firewall
→ Corporate Firewall
→ Microsoft Defender Cloud Service
Components
1. Domain Controller
Hosts Active Directory Services and the Defender Sensor
2. Defender for Identity Sensor
Captures and analyzes authentication and network traffic
3. Firewall Layer
o Windows Firewall on DC
o Perimeter firewall
o Azure NSG (for Azure DCs)
4. Defender Cloud Service
Receives telemetry data for security analysis
Network and port requirements
Domain Controllers with sensor require:
• Outbound HTTPS connection (TCP 443)
• DNS resolution of external Microsoft endpoints
• Access to specific Defender Service URLs
• Internal AD ports (LDAP, Kerberos, RPC) unchanged
Blocked or filtered connections result in “unhealthy” status.
Operational principles
• Sensor installation must be performed consistently on all DCs
• Firewall rules must be configured according to Microsoft recommendations
• Changes to network segments must be reviewed
• After agent reinstallation, health status must be monitored
• Parallel installation of multiple sensors per DC is not permitted
decision tree & use cases
Initial question
Is only a single Domain Controller affected or multiple?
A) Only one DC affected
1. Is the Defender Sensor service running?
2. Is outbound HTTPS connectivity available (Port 443)?
3. Is DNS resolution to Microsoft Defender endpoints successful?
4. Check event logs (Sensor / network errors)
5. If installation is inconsistent → remove agent and reinstall
B) Multiple DCs affected
1. Has a firewall or NSG rule been changed?
2. Has a network segment been redefined?
3. Has a proxy or TLS policy been modified?
4. Is general internet connectivity available?
C) Sensor repeatedly switches from “healthy” to “unhealthy”
1. Check for temporary network interruption
2. Check TLS or certificate issues
3. Check proxy authentication
4. Check agent version
D) Issue persists after reinstallation
1. Verify that firewall rules are complete
2. Check whether Azure NSG blocks outbound connections
3. Check Defender Service Health Portal
4. Is internal security software blocking telemetry?

Helpdesk checks firewall:
• Rule for port 443 present
• NAT correct
• No blocking visible in logs
Load balancer status:
Backend server marked as “Healthy”.
03.06.25 11:40
Test with direct IP to Reverse Proxy → works.
Test via FQDN → 502
Notable:
Header “X-Forwarded-Proto” missing in external request.
04.06.25 09:05
Customer asks whether certificate may be incorrectly bound.
Certificate validation:
• CN correct
• SAN correct
• Chain complete
No obvious errors.
05.06.25 08:50
Ticket forwarded to Network / Security Team.
Assigned group changed to: BU Network & Security
06.06.25 09:30
Taken over by System Engineer 21
WAF analysis:
• Policy active
• TLS inspection active
• HTTP/2 enabled
Notable:
After patch, default profile was reloaded.
06.06.25 11:15
Test: TLS inspection temporarily disabled →
Login works.
Inspection re-enabled →
502 again.
09.06.25 10:05
Further analysis:
App server expects header:
X-Forwarded-Proto: https
When TLS inspection is active, header is not forwarded.
09.06.25 14:40
Comparison with architecture documentation performed.
Reference in internal document:
“Blueprint: Secure Web Publishing – KF40 – Version 3.2”
Section 4.3 describes special header forwarding in case of TLS offloading.
10.06.25 08:25
Blueprint document loaded from DMS.
Relevant notes:
• TLS termination must occur on WAF
• Backend expects HTTPS signaling
• Custom header rewrite rule required
• Inspection policy must not remove HTTP headers
• Specific exception defined for portal application
10.06.25 11:45
Configuration comparison with blueprint shows:
• Custom header rewrite rule missing
• HTTP profile does not match documented version
• Inspection template “Default-Strict” active instead of “Portal-App-Template”
12.06.25
Internal coordination:
Firewall patch overwrote template.
Custom policy was not reassigned.
16.06.25
Configuration reviewed and adjusted according to blueprint.
No technical details documented in ticket (security policy).
18.06.25 14:55
External access test successful.
Multiple login attempts tested.
Monitoring stable for 24 hours.
Ticket closure
18.06.25 15:33
Dear Contact Person 19
The root cause was a deviation in the Reverse Proxy configuration from the documented architectural specification following a security patch.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal blueprint documentation.
The customer portal is now fully accessible again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Activities
10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25
Good morning
At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails.
Internal recipients within our company receive the scans normally.
Printing continues to function without issue.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
10.12.25 08:41
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30.
10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis
Test scan to internal mailbox → successful.
Test scan to external address → no delivery.
No error message visible on the printer itself.
10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network?
10.12.25 10:40 – Customer response
Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted.
Additionally, new DNS forwarding was configured at the location.
11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis
Internal delivery occurs directly.
External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay.
SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”.
11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail:
• Printers must not send directly to the internet
• Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay
• Relay allows only defined IP ranges
• DNS resolution must occur internally
• Authentication must not be bypassed
12.12.25 – Further analysis
Check of printer IP address.
Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN.
This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay.
Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked.
15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team
Confirmation that the new printer VLAN was not included in the relay allow list.
16.12.25 – Adjustment
SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint.
IP range of printer VLAN released.
17.12.25 – Test phase
Scan to internal mailbox → successful
Scan to external Gmail address → successful
Scan to partner company → successful
18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 25
The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Helpdesk checks:

Notable:
“Compliance_Core” is not identical to “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”.
However, according to SharePoint, permission is still managed via “SP_Compliance_Main”.
Unclear correlation.
21.03.25 16:10
Ticket remains open due to inconsistent behavior.
Status: In Progress
22.03.25 09:05
Internal note – Employee 27
Since the behavior remains inconsistent (partial access, partial denial, partial access only in Incognito mode), the ticket is escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – M365 / Identity
22.03.25 09:12
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 11
Status: In Progress
22.03.25 10:25
Analysis by System Engineer 11
Step 1 – Sensitivity Label review
Label “Confidential – Internal” checked:
• Encryption active
• Access only for members of Azure AD group “Compliance_Core”
• Automatic inheritance for folders enabled
Notable:
Folder “Compliance_Review_2025” has Unique Permissions.
Label was applied AFTER renaming.
Hypothesis: Conflict between SharePoint permissions and AIP label encryption.
22.03.25 11:40
Test:
• User added directly to “Compliance_Core”
→ Access immediately possible
After removal from “Compliance_Core”
→ Access denied again
However:
Other users WITHOUT “Compliance_Core” membership have access.
Hypothesis rejected – not purely group-based.
24.03.25 08:55
Analysis Azure AD Sign-In Logs
For affected users:
• Token contains claim for “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• No claim for “Compliance_Core”
• Conditional Access evaluation successful
No blocking visible.
24.03.25 10:10
In-depth review via Microsoft Purview / Information Protection
Notable:
When applying the Sensitivity Label, the option
“Preserve custom permissions”
was not activated.
Result:
• Existing SharePoint permissions formally remained
• Encryption, however, applies only to explicitly defined groups
This creates a situation where:
SharePoint says → Access allowed
AIP encryption says → Access only with key permission
Depending on token refresh timing, access is granted or blocked.
24.03.25 14:35
Reproduction in test tenant:
• Folder with Unique Permissions created
• Sensitivity Label applied afterward
• Different users tested
Result:
Users with older access tokens could temporarily access.
After token renewal, access blocked.
25.03.25 09:15
Root cause defined:
The folder was:
1. Renamed
2. Assigned Unique Permissions
3. Subsequently encrypted with a Sensitivity Label
The encryption defined a different permission group than the SharePoint ACL.
Token caching caused inconsistent behavior.
25.03.25 11:40 – Actions
• Sensitivity Label removed
• Folder reassigned with correct group reference
• Label reapplied with correct group integration
• “Compliance_Core” and “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” structurally aligned
• Tests performed with multiple user accounts
• Tokens intentionally invalidated
26.03.25 – Monitoring
Multiple users tested
Access consistently possible
No further error messages
28.03.25 15:42 – Customer feedback
Audit team confirms stable access for all employees.
Ticket closure
28.03.25 16:02
Dear Contact Person 02
The root cause was an inconsistent combination of Unique SharePoint permissions and a subsequently applied Sensitivity Label encryption.
Due to differing group references between ACL and encryption, inconsistent access behavior occurred, amplified by token caching.
The permission structure was aligned and tested.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

title: Blueprint_AD FS.txt
text:
﻿Blueprint: Identity & Authentication (AD FS / Entra ID Integration)
Version / History
Version | Date | Author | Notes
0.1 | 11.02.2026 | PS | Creation (Initial)
0.2 | – | – | –
General Information and Description
This is the blueprint for Identity & Authentication (AD FS / Entra ID Integration).
Active Directory Federation Services (AD FS) is an identity service provided by Microsoft that enables organizations to centrally manage authentication and authorization processes and to provide Single Sign-On (SSO) for both internal and external applications.
AD FS is commonly used in hybrid IT environments where on-premises applications, cloud services, and external partners are integrated.
In combination with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory), AD FS enables the issuance of security tokens (e.g., SAML or JWT/OpenID Connect), which are used by target applications for authentication and authorization. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) mechanisms can optionally be integrated, either directly via AD FS or through connected claims providers.
A key characteristic of this architecture is that different authentication flows may exist:
• Browser-based logins (e.g., web interfaces)
• Non-browser-based logins (e.g., services, integrations, APIs)
As a result, it is possible for a login via browser to succeed while an application or integration fails to connect despite correct credentials. In such cases, the root cause is often inconsistent client parameters, token format incompatibilities, or implicit MFA delegations that are not immediately visible during standard troubleshooting.
This blueprint describes the fundamental architecture, the relevant design decisions, and typical integration scenarios for AD FS-based authentication.
Licensing
The following licensing principles apply to this architecture:
• Microsoft Entra ID Free / P1 / P2
(depending on required features such as Conditional Access or MFA)
• Windows Server license for operating AD FS
• No additional cloud license is required specifically for AD FS
The specific licensing model depends on the scope of authentication, the number of users, and security requirements (e.g., MFA, Conditional Access).
Best Practices
Our best practices are aligned with Microsoft’s official recommendations and include in particular:
• Clear separation between browser-based and application-based authentication
• Deliberate choice of SAML or JWT (OIDC) per application
• Documentation of all client registrations and parameters
• Careful use of MFA delegation to external claims providers
• Regular review of certificates and token signatures









Desing



Decision tree & use cases
Decision Tree – Authentication Issues
AD FS / Entra ID / MFA
1. 
Question: Can users successfully sign in via a browser-based interface (e.g., web login)?
• Yes: Proceed to question 2.
• No: Check the AD FS service and the user account. Then reassess the ticket.
2. 
Question: Does the issue occur with an application or integration without a browser (e.g., service, API, background process)?
• Yes: Proceed to question 3.
• No: Check the application configuration outside of this blueprint.
3. 
Question: Do you know which authentication protocol the application uses?
• OpenID Connect / OAuth2 (JWT): Proceed to question 4.
• SAML: Proceed to question 6.
• Unknown: Clarify the authentication mechanism with the application owner.
4. 
Question: Is the client correctly registered in AD FS (Client ID, Redirect URI)?
• Yes: Proceed to question 5.
• No: Correct the client registration and test again.
5. 
Question: Is the application designed to process JWT tokens?
• Yes: Proceed to question 7.
• No: Check whether a switch to SAML is required or whether the application must be adapted.
6. 
Question: Do other SAML-based applications work with the same AD FS configuration?
• Yes: Check the specific SAML configuration of the affected application.
• No: Check the general SAML configuration in AD FS.
7. 
Question: Is Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) enabled in this sign-in process?
• Yes: Proceed to question 8.
• No: Proceed to question 9.
8. 
Question: Is MFA requested via an external claims provider?
• Yes: Check whether the application supports MFA.
• No: Check the MFA configuration directly in AD FS.
9. 
Question: Does the error occur only sporadically or only for specific users?
• Yes: Check certificates, time deviations, and existing sessions.
• No: Proceed to question 10.
10. 
Question: Was the issue resolved by adjusting AD FS parameters or client settings?
• Yes: Document the change and close the ticket.
• No: Escalate the case to the Identity or Architecture team.
Use Cases
Use Case 1 – Login works in the browser but not in the application
A user can sign in via the web interface, but a connected application or integration fails.
The decision tree helps to isolate the difference between browser-based and application-based authentication.
Use Case 2 – Authentication error after configuration change
After adjustments to AD FS or client settings, new sign-in errors occur.
The decision tree supports systematically reviewing the relevant parameters.
Use Case 3 – Intermittent or user-specific errors
Sign-in works only intermittently or only for certain users.
The decision tree guides targeted checks of certificates, time deviations, and session data.
Use Case 4 – Escalation decision
If no clear root cause can be identified, the decision tree helps determine when escalation is required and when further analysis is appropriate.

18.09.25 11:40 – Internal analysis
Test call from private mobile (different number range) → works.
Test with number in specified range → call not delivered.
In the Teams Admin Center, no incoming call is shown.
19.09.25 09:05 – Initial hypothesis
Possible provider issue.
Carrier contacted.
19.09.25 14:20 – Carrier feedback
From the carrier’s perspective, the call is correctly delivered to the company’s SBC.
No routing errors visible on their side.
22.09.25 08:55 – SBC log analysis
For functioning numbers:
INVITE accepted and forwarded to Teams.
For affected numbers:
INVITE received but immediately answered with “404 Not Found”.
22.09.25 11:30 – Email to customer
Good day
We see that incoming calls from the provider are reaching the SBC.
However, the requests are not being correctly forwarded internally.
Were there recent changes to number ranges or new extensions?
Kind regards
22.09.25 13:05 – Customer response
Yes, we received a new number range from the provider last week and partially migrated old numbers.
The affected numbers belong to this new range.
23.09.25 09:10 – Internal review
Comparison with document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Number Translation – Version 2.3”
Section regarding:
• Normalization of incoming numbers
• E.164 format
• Translation rules on SBC
• Voice routing policies
Finding:
New numbers are not included in the SBC translation table.
Routing rule covers only the old number range.
24.09.25 – Test
Temporary adjustment of translation rule for new number range.
Test call performed.
Call correctly signaled and delivered.
Reverting to original configuration → issue reproducible.
25.09.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that new number range was fully migrated.
Internal documentation had not yet been updated.
26.09.25 – Adjustment
Routing and translation rules extended according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
29.09.25 – Customer feedback
All tested numbers are working again.
Multiple customers also confirmed successful calls.
Thank you for the support.
29.09.25 16:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 22
The root cause was an inconsistent translation and routing configuration following the introduction of a new number range.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Incoming calls are now correctly delivered again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

title: Blueprint_sharepoint.txt
text:
﻿
general info & description
SharePoint Permission & Group Architecture – Customer Company 25
Version: 1.0
Date: 20.06.2025
Author: M365 / Identity Engineering
Notes: Initial creation based on project structure IT-Projects
This document describes the architectural and governance specifications for permission structures in SharePoint Online at Customer Company 25 AG.
The objective is a consistent, maintainable, and auditable permission structure for project folders within SharePoint document libraries.
Core principles:
• No direct user permissions at folder or library level
• Permissions exclusively via dedicated Azure AD groups
• Clear separation between functional groups and SharePoint resource groups
• Documented group nesting
• No parallel or historical groups with identical purpose
Group model
A distinction is made between two group types:
1. Functional groups
Example:
IT-Projects
Finance-Team
Risk-Team
2. SharePoint resource groups
Example:
SP_Project_IT
SP_Project_IT_Lead
Functional groups must never receive permissions directly in SharePoint.
They are nested into dedicated SharePoint groups.
Group lifecycle
• New project groups must be uniquely named
• Historical groups (e.g., *_v1) must be structurally removed before deactivation
• No parallel existence of old and new resource groups
• Group changes must be documented
MUST BE OBSERVED:
SharePoint evaluates effective permissions based on the current group nesting in Entra ID.
Incorrectly nested groups result in missing effective permissions.
design
Architecture overview
User
→ Member of functional group
→ Functional group member in SharePoint resource group
→ SharePoint resource group has folder permission
Permission model
Folder or library permissions are assigned exclusively to SharePoint resource groups.
Example:
Folder: /Projects/2025_Migration
Authorized: SP_Project_IT
Structure:
IT-Projects
└── SP_Project_IT
SP_Project_IT_Lead
└── SP_Project_IT
Unique Permissions
Unique Permissions may only be set if:
• It is documented which resource group is referenced
• No old or deactivated group is still nested
• The target group is clearly active
When creating a new resource group, it must be verified:
• Whether the old version is still referenced
• Whether the nesting has been updated
• Whether users are authorized only indirectly and not directly
Synchronization & Propagation
After changes to groups:
• Wait for synchronization time
• Verify effective permissions
• Do not allow parallel groups with identical scope
decision tree & use cases
1. Question: Is SharePoint Online generally accessible?
No → The issue is not related to the permission model.
Yes → Proceed to question 2.
2. Question: Does access work for other users?
No → Check folder permissions.
Yes → Proceed to question 3.
3. Question: Is the affected user directly a member of the resource group?
Yes → The issue is not related to nesting.
No → Proceed to question 4.
4. Question: Is the functional group correctly nested in the resource group?
No → Review group structure.
Yes → Proceed to question 5.
5. Question: Do old or deactivated groups with similar names exist?
Yes → Consolidate group structure.
No → Further analysis required.

title: Ticketzusammenfassung_printscan3.txt
text:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs
Ticket number: TI-1622483
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 15
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 06.11.25 08:37
Closure
Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52
Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored.
Activities
06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15
Good morning
Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed.
Other printers in the building are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
06.11.25 08:37
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21.
06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis
Remote access to an affected user.
Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing.
Queue itself is not paused.
Print Spooler service is running.
Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails.
06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location?
Thank you for your brief feedback.
06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response
Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN.
No other changes are known to us.
07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis
Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful.
Ping from a client within the same location network successful.
This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN.
Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly.
07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team
Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN.
Rule adjusted according to standard.
10.11.25 – Test phase
Printing from print server successful.
Multiple test prints from user workstations performed.
No abnormalities observed.
12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback
Printer is functioning normally again.
Larger print jobs are also processed correctly.
12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 15
The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

title: blueprint_sap.txt
text:
﻿
general info & description
Print Services for SAP Output Standard
Version 1.0
Date: 25.07.2024
Status: Approved
This document describes the standard for the provisioning of SAP outputs (e.g., delivery notes, pick lists, labels) via central Windows print servers at Customer Company 13. The objective is a stable operation of SAP output processes, as printing disruptions can have direct impact on logistics and deliveries.
The standard covers the technical provisioning of SAP-relevant print queues on dedicated print servers as well as the operational guidelines following Windows updates, restores, or maintenance windows. Special focus is placed on the consistency of printer queue configuration (Queue Properties) and network sharing and publishing mechanisms, as these may be relevant for SAP print paths and spool processing.
Typical symptoms of deviations from this standard include: print jobs remaining stuck in SAP, print jobs disappearing from the queue without printing, or print jobs accumulating on the print server and only being released after manual intervention. This pattern was observed in ticket TI-931293 after a Windows update and subsequent VM restore.
design
Architecture overview
SAP System
→ SAP Spool / Output Management
→ SAP print target (logical printer definition)
→ Windows Printserver (SAP Printserver)
→ Print queues
→ Physical printers
Roles and components
1. SAP Output Management
Generates print jobs and transfers them to defined print targets. The availability and correct processing of print targets is business-critical.
2. SAP Printserver (Windows)
Serves as the central processing instance for SAP printing. The relevant queues are operated on dedicated print servers (in the ticket: VRINFRGERPRNT03 and VRINFRGERPRNT04).
3. Print queues
Queue properties must be standardized and consistent. After system changes (updates, restores), it must be ensured that publishing/sharing parameters and queue status are correct and that no unintended states (e.g., stuck jobs) exist.
4. Print Spooler Service
The service is responsible for processing print jobs. A restart may temporarily influence symptoms but does not necessarily resolve configuration deviations at queue level.
Operational principles
• After Windows updates or VM restores, a validation of the SAP print servers must be performed (queue status, spooler, sharing/publishing of queues, test print from SAP).
• SAP print paths must be treated as business-critical; disruptions are to be handled with priority (classified as PRIO HIGH in the ticket).
• If printing issues occur after update/restore, queue configuration must be treated as the primary checkpoint, not only the spooler service.
decision tree & use cases
Decision Tree
Initial question: Does the issue affect only printing from SAP or also normal printing (e.g., Office)?
A) Only SAP printing affected, normal printing works
A1: Are all SAP print targets affected or only specific print servers?
• Only specific print servers affected → Perform SAP printserver-specific checks (queue status, publishing/sharing, spooler, restore state)
• All print servers affected → Check SAP output configuration (print targets, spool queue, SAP-side errors)
A2: Was there a Windows update or VM restore within the last 24–72 hours?
• Yes → Perform post-change validation according to standard (queue properties, sharing/publishing, test print)
• No → Proceed to A3
A3: Are SAP print jobs visible on the print server (queue) or do they remain stuck in SAP?
• Remain stuck in SAP → Check SAP output / connection to print target
• Visible on the print server but no print output → Check queue/spooler/sharing mechanism
B) SAP printing and normal printing affected
B1: Is the Print Spooler service running?
• No → Start service / check service recovery
• Yes → Proceed to B2
B2: Are queues paused or are jobs permanently stuck?
• Yes → Correct queue status, clear stuck jobs, then perform test print
• No → Check network path / printer reachability
C) Issue occurs after update/restore and is printserver-specific
C1: Check queue properties according to standard
• Validate publishing/sharing status of printer queues
• If inconsistent state: disable/enable relevant queue parameters as a reset measure
This procedure corresponds to the pattern documented in the ticket, where after update and restore the network sharing/queue functionality had to be corrected.

title: Ticketzusammenfassung_sharepoint4.txt
text:
﻿
Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – External project members lose access sporadically
Ticket number: TI-1583327
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 31
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.04.25 08:12
Closure
Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18
Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable.
Activities
07.04.25 08:04
Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint
From: Contact Person 07
Good morning
Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Internal employees do not appear to have issues.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 07
07.04.25 08:12
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 31
Status: In Progress
Notes
07.04.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external users?
• What error message appears?
• Since when exactly?
• Were groups or permissions adjusted?
07.04.25 10:20
Customer responds:
Error message:
Access denied
One user could access yesterday.
Today no longer.
No deliberate changes made.
07.04.25 11:10
Helpdesk checks SharePoint:
• Folder permissions correct
• Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Comparison internal vs external users:
No visible difference.
08.04.25 08:45
Customer adds:
One external user was newly added and immediately had access.
Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically.
08.04.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests:
• Remove + re-add to group
→ Access temporarily restored
After approx. 1 hour, no access again.
08.04.25 14:30
Hypothesis:
Token / cache / Conditional Access
Test:
User fully logs out → logs in again
→ Access remains denied
Hypothesis rejected.
09.04.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”?
09.04.25 10:05
Customer:
All externals are created as Guest.
Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud.
09.04.25 11:40
First observation:
For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD
Correlation unclear.
09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket number: TI-1621089

Colleagues in the same office see the printer normally.
Could you please check this?

User account checked.
User is a member of the correct AD group for location-based printers.

gpupdate /force executed and user logged in again.
